channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world

item.title: Israeli defense minister warns an attack on Iran would be 'lethal' and 'surprising'
item.description: JERUSALEM (AP) — Israel's defense minister warned on Wednesday that his country's retaliation for a recent Iranian missile attack will be “lethal” and “surprising,” while the Israeli military pushed ahead with a large-scale operation in northern Gaza and a ground offensive in Lebanon against Hezboll
item.dc:creator: TIA GOLDENBERG, SAMY MAGDY and WAFAA SHURAFA, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Mozambique counts votes in its presidential election as opposition alleges fraud
item.description: MAPUTO, Mozambique (AP) — Mozambique began counting votes late Wednesday in a presidential election that is expected to extend the ruling party's 49 years in power, though the opposition was already alleging fraud and manipulation.
item.dc:creator: CHARLES MANGWIRO, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Kremlin confirms Trump sent COVID tests to Putin during pandemic
item.description: “We also sent equipment at the beginning of the pandemic,” Kremlin spokesman Dmitry Peskov said. “But about the phone calls — it’s not true.
item.dc:creator: Laura Kelly, NewsNation

item.title: North Korea vows to block border with South Korea and build front-line defense structures
item.description: To cope with 'confrontational hysteria' by U.S. and South Korean forces, North Korea said it will block its border with South Korea permanently and will cut off railways and roads to the country.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Zelenskyy will present his Ukraine 'victory plan' to Western leaders but Biden meeting is postponed
item.description: DUBROVNIK, Croatia (AP) — Ukraine's president on Wednesday said he intends to present his “victory plan” to Western leaders this week, but a weekend meeting to discuss it has been postponed as U.S. President Joe Biden said he had to stay home to respond to Hurricane Milton’ s landfall in Florida.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Trump 'God Bless the USA' Bibles printed in China, records show
item.description: Thousands of copies of former President Trump's "God Bless the USA" Bible were printed in China, according to trade records reviewed by The Associated Press. According to AP, the records showed a printing company in eastern China shipped nearly 120,000 of the Trump Bibles to the U.S.
item.dc:creator: Juliann Ventura, The Hill

item.title: Language, lack of money and fear are matters of life and death with Milton approaching Florida
item.description: Hurricane Milton is expected to unleash its greatest force over hundreds of thousands of immigrants who don’t speak English.
item.dc:creator: Gisela Salomon, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: The history of the Hoxne Hoard, the largest collection of Roman treasure found in Britain
item.description: The Hoxne Hoard is one of the most significant archaeological finds out of Britain. The hoard is mostly made of gold and silver coins, although other unique objects add to its value.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Israel hit with multiple terrorist attacks as Hezbollah rocket kills 2 Israelis while walking dogs
item.description: Israel was hit with another round of terrorist attacks as Hezbollah fired rockets into a northern border town, killing two, and knife-wielding moped driver attacked in Hadera, putting eight in the hospital.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Elon Musk's X is back in Brazil after its suspension, having complied with all judicial demands
item.description: The social media platform X is returning to Brazil after remaining inaccessible for more than a month following a clash between its owner, Elon Musk, and a justice on the country’s highest court.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Japan's new PM dissolves the lower house of parliament to set up a snap election
item.description: TOKYO (AP) — New Japanese Prime Minister Shigeru Ishiba dissolved the lower house of parliament Wednesday to set up an Oct. 27 snap election, asking people to trust the governing party's policies even as critics said the vote comes far too soon.
item.dc:creator: MARI YAMAGUCHI, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: North Korea vows to block border with South Korea and build front-line defense structures
item.description: SEOUL, South Korea (AP) — North Korea said Wednesday it will permanently block its border with South Korea and build front-line defense structures to cope with “confrontational hysteria" by South Korean and U.S.
item.dc:creator: HYUNG-JIN KIM, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Survivors of massacre in small Haitian town where 70 died point finger at government
item.description: PONT-SONDÉ, Haiti (AP) — Angry whispers have broken the heavy silence that fell over Pont-Sondé just days after a vicious gang attack left more than 70 dead, marking one of Haiti’s biggest massacres in recent history.
item.dc:creator: PIERRE-RICHARD LUXAMA and DÁNICA COTO, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Hezbollah steps up rocket fire as Israel sends more troops into Lebanon
item.description: BEIRUT (AP) — Hezbollah fired another barrage of rockets into Israel on Tuesday, and the militant group’s acting leader vowed to keep up pressure that has forced tens of thousands of Israelis from their homes near the Lebanese border.
item.dc:creator: BASSEM MROUE and TIA GOLDENBERG, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Musk's X to be reinstated in Brazil after complying with Supreme Court demands
item.description: SAO PAULO (AP) — The Brazilian Supreme Court's Justice Alexandre de Moraes on Tuesday authorized the restoration of social media platform X´s service in Brazil, over a month after its nationwide shutdown, according to a court document that was made public. Elon Musk’s X was blocked on Aug.
item.dc:creator: GABRIELA SÁ PESSOA and BARBARA ORTUTAY, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Dominican Republic starts mass deportations of Haitians and expels nearly 11,000 in a week
item.description: SANTO DOMINGO, Dominican Republic (AP) — The Dominican Republic said Tuesday it has deported or repatriated nearly 11,000 Haitians in the past week, fulfilling a pledge to do so weekly as neighboring Haiti scrambles to handle the influx while besieged by gang violence and poverty.
item.dc:creator: MARTÍN ADAMES ALCÁNTARA and DÁNICA COTO, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Netanyahu says replacement for slain Hezbollah chief was killed
item.description: Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu said Tuesday that his forces have killed the replacement for slain Hezbollah leader Hassan Nasrallah in another blow for the Lebanese militant group.
item.dc:creator: Brad Dress, The Hill

item.title: Netanyahu confirms Nasrallah's replacement dead: ‘Thousands of terrorists’ killed
item.description: Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu confirmed the death of the Hezbollah leader slated to replace Sayyed Hassan Nasrallah and warned the Lebanese people to oust the terrorist group or endure more war.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: MI5 spy chief says Russia and Iran are behind a 'staggering' rise in deadly plots
item.description: LONDON (AP) — Britain is facing a “staggering rise” in attempts at assassination, sabotage and other crimes on U.K. soil by Russia and Iran, as the two states recruit criminals to “do their dirty work,” the head of Britain's domestic intelligence agency said Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: JILL LAWLESS, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Suspect in Madeleine McCann disappearance case acquitted of unrelated sexual offense charges
item.description: A German court has acquitted Christian Brueckner, who is also under investigation in the disappearance of British toddler Madeleine McCann, in a trial over unrelated sexual offenses.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: German court acquits McCann suspect of unrelated sexual offense charges
item.description: BRAUNSCHWEIG, Germany (AP) — A German court on Tuesday acquitted a man who is also under investigation in the 2007 disappearance of British toddler Madeleine McCann in a trial on charges of unrelated sexual offenses.
item.dc:creator: FANNY BRODERSEN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Water gushes through palm trees and sand dunes after rare rain in the Sahara Desert
item.description: RABAT, Morocco (AP) — A rare deluge of rainfall left blue lagoons of water amid the palm trees and sand dunes of the Sahara desert, nourishing some of its most drought-stricken regions with more water than many had seen in decades.
item.dc:creator: SAM METZ and BABA AHMED, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Mexican mayor murdered less than a week after taking office
item.description: Mayor Alejandro Arcos of Chilpancingo, Mexico, has been confirmed dead less than a week after being sworn into the notoriously dangerous city's highest office.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Four American hostages remain in Hamas captivity a year after Oct. 7 massacre
item.description: Secretary of State Antony Blinken is vowing that the U.S. "will continue to work tirelessly" to bring home Americans being held hostage by Hamas in Gaza.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: How many are dead, missing one year since Hamas' attack on Israel?
item.description: Oct. 7 marks the first anniversary of Hamas’ attack on Israel, which prompted an Israeli invasion of Gaza.
item.dc:creator: Ashley Soriano, NewsNation

item.title: Russian arms dealer Viktor Bout, who was traded for Brittney Griner, to sell weapons to Iran-backed Houthis
item.description: Russian "Merchant of Death" Viktor Bout has reportedly resumed his role as an arms dealer following his December 2022 release from U.S. custody.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Saving lives on 'Death Street,' how an Israeli kindergarten teacher became a battlefield hero on October 7
item.description: When Hamas terrorists attacked her hometown in Southern Israel, Tali Hadad a kindergarten teacher, leapt into action to save 13 people, including her own wounded son.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: American father of Hamas hostage Itay Chen pushes US, Israel on ‘Plan B’ as negotiations falter
item.description: The father of an American held by Hamas is urging the Biden administration to think outside of the box and return his son and others from Gaza as Israel's year-long strategy fails to free them.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Abigail Edan's aunt says 'we need a deal' on Oct. 7 anniversary
item.description: "I say this to the Palestinian people, and I say this to the Israelis Diplomacy now is what we need," Naftali said.
item.dc:creator: Anna Kutz, NewsNation

item.title: Iran lifts flight restrictions ahead of schedule after grounding all flights for 'operational restrictions'
item.description: Flight restrictions were lifted in Iran after safe conditions were confirmed, state media reported on Monday, shortening the period of flight cancellations announced earlier.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Middle East latest: Israeli strike in Beirut killed 7 health and rescue workers
item.description: An Israeli strike overnight in the Lebanese capital Beirut killed seven health and rescue workers, an Islamic health organization said Thursday.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, NewsNation
